flags.rewound_from_stage0_5_pivot: case 6b — boundary is stage_0.5_to_0, reviewer status is reject on conjecture-level grounds (kernel_substituted, definitional flagship, no generic-class obstruction), not a localized derivation defect; re-deriving on the same .tex would burn budget on a proposal whose headline theorem is tautological by construction.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of the local average treatment effect under a continuous instrument when the Imbens-Angrist monotonicity restriction is relaxed to a calibrated bounded-violation regime — the share of the population whose first-stage response is non-monotonic in the instrument is bounded above by a known propensity-derivative envelope — characterizing the sharp identified set of LATE as a function of the violation budget and the observed propensity-derivative function, recovering Heckman-Vytlacil MTE point identification at the trivial bound, and proving a strict-gap structural separation theorem against the standard quasi-monotone IV identifying formula
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue/banked prior art remains pid_late_bounded_defiers_one_sided_compliance, pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1, pid_dose_response_iv_v1, and pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v1. The revised proposal does not repackage those finite LP gaps: it makes the continuous derivative-path pasting problem itself the target through K_b^proj and a support-function certificate in a specified bounded-Lipschitz product space.","published_axis":"The closest published papers are Kitagawa2015, ChesherRosen2017, KilbertusKusnerSilva2020, Gunsilius2019, and HeckmanVytlacil2005. The epsilon-gap is a scalar continuous-IV derivative-process feasibility theorem under a fixed function-valued negative-response envelope, including finite-threshold support certificates and subdistribution-in-y constraints; none of the listed papers gives this bounded-violation derivative-path certificate."}
seed_list: Prior work HeckmanVytlacil2005 has established that the derivative ratio m'(z)/p'(z) identifies an MTE under threshold crossing. Our seed conjectures that a sharp signed-response projection replaces that point formula when a negative first-stage derivative density is bounded, because local defier mass must be pasted into one bounded-variation response path.

Prior work Klein2010 would predict that monotonicity violations enter continuous-IV estimands as bias terms around the derivative formula. Our seed conjectures that the quasi-monotone derivative ratio is generically separated from the sharp positive-responder identified set, because signed first-stage cancellation can hide outcome heterogeneity between positive and negative responders.

Prior work Vytlacil2002 has established that LATE monotonicity is equivalent to a threshold-crossing latent-index model. Our seed conjectures that a bounded negative derivative envelope defines an intermediate response-path class with sharp feasibility inequalities, because the path can have bounded downward variation without admitting any threshold index.

Prior work ImbensAngrist1994 has established that instrument validity plus monotonicity identifies a complier effect. Our seed conjectures that instrument validity plus a continuous negative-response envelope yields a nontrivial interval whose endpoints are attained by extreme signed-response laws, because compliers and defiers are locally inseparable through p' alone.

Prior work Kitagawa2015 has established testable implications of IV validity and monotonicity for discrete instruments. Our seed conjectures that continuous-IV bounded violations have derivative-process feasibility certificates, because a latent response path imposes cone restrictions on (p',F'_{Y,D|Z}) beyond pointwise envelope bounds.

Prior work MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2021 has established that partial monotonicity can restore positive-weight IV interpretation with multiple instruments. Our seed conjectures that scalar continuous-IV envelope monotonicity has a different positive-weight object, because the negative derivative density is calibrated along one margin rather than removed by instrument-by-instrument sign restrictions.

Prior work DeChaisemartin2017 has established that LATE-like effects without full monotonicity require extra structure tying defiers to compliers. Our seed conjectures that a bounded continuous-IV defier density gives sharp intervals without tying their outcomes, because the envelope controls mass while leaving treatment-effect heterogeneity adversarial.

Prior work Kitagawa2015 would imply that monotonicity failures can be detected by finite conditional-distribution inequalities in discrete IV models. Our seed conjectures that continuous-IV envelope refutability is governed by derivative cone inequalities, because infinitesimal response signs must integrate into a feasible bounded-variation binary path.

Prior work HeckmanVytlacil2005 has established pointwise LIV/MTE identification under monotone threshold crossing. Our seed conjectures that the bounded-violation sharp set converges to the LIV formula in Hausdorff distance as b -> 0 only under first-stage strength, because weak p' regions can amplify vanishing negative response mass.

Prior work pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1 asserted strict pairwise-versus-global tightening only as a J=2 witness problem. Our seed conjectures that the continuum analogue creates a mesh-refinement strictness theorem, because adjacent continuous-IV envelopes cannot be optimized independently when one response path must serve all margins.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HeckmanVytlacil2005 has established that the derivative ratio m'(z)/p'(z) identifies an MTE under threshold crossing. Our seed conjectures that a sharp signed-response projection replaces that point formula when a negative first-stage derivative density is bounded, because local defier mass must be pasted into one bounded-variation response path.

anchor_paper.author: James J. Heckman and Edward Vytlacil
anchor_paper.claim: The MTE/LIV formula identifies marginal treatment effects from local derivatives of observed outcome and propensity functions under threshold crossing.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked because the positive envelope changes point identification into a signed-measure sharp-set problem with a global pasting obstruction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from HeckmanVytlacil2005 because their LIV derivative already describes the continuous-IV margin.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Heckman-Vytlacil impose threshold crossing, so their derivative has no negative response measure or bounded-violation sharpness/pasting problem.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Klein2010 would predict that monotonicity violations enter continuous-IV estimands as bias terms around the derivative formula. Our seed conjectures that the quasi-monotone derivative ratio is generically separated from the sharp positive-responder identified set, because signed first-stage cancellation can hide outcome heterogeneity between positive and negative responders.

anchor_paper.author: Tobias J. Klein
anchor_paper.claim: Local departures from monotonicity alter monotonicity-based MTE, ATE, and LATE estimands through bias terms rather than preserving the point-identification formula.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/separation pattern, not blocked because the refuted object is the named published monotonicity-based derivative formula and the target is an open class of observables.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Klein2010 because Klein already derives bias from monotonicity violations.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Klein gives bias approximations, not a strict distance theorem between a sharp bounded-violation identified set and the derivative-ratio formula.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Vytlacil2002 has established that LATE monotonicity is equivalent to a threshold-crossing latent-index model. Our seed conjectures that a bounded negative derivative envelope defines an intermediate response-path class with sharp feasibility inequalities, because the path can have bounded downward variation without admitting any threshold index.

anchor_paper.author: Edward J. Vytlacil
anchor_paper.claim: The LATE monotonicity condition is equivalent to a latent-index threshold-crossing selection model under the maintained IV assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption-inversion pattern, not blocked because the mechanism is bounded downward variation as a distinct response-path geometry, not a generic weakening of monotonicity.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Vytlacil2002 because failure of monotonicity is exactly failure of threshold crossing.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: equivalence identifies the zero-violation corner but does not characterize calibrated bounded violation classes or sharp observables.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ImbensAngrist1994 has established that instrument validity plus monotonicity identifies a complier effect. Our seed conjectures that instrument validity plus a continuous negative-response envelope yields a nontrivial interval whose endpoints are attained by extreme signed-response laws, because compliers and defiers are locally inseparable through p' alone.

anchor_paper.author: Guido W. Imbens and Joshua D. Angrist
anchor_paper.claim: LATE point identification requires an instrument plus monotonicity; instrument validity alone does not identify the complier average effect.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens-assumption pattern, not blocked because the relaxation introduces a sharp endpoint geometry and signed cancellation, not just a broader hypothesis class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ImbensAngrist1994 because without monotonicity LATE is not point identified.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: non-point-identification is known, but the continuous-envelope sharp interval as a function of b and p' is not.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Kitagawa2015 has established testable implications of IV validity and monotonicity for discrete instruments. Our seed conjectures that continuous-IV bounded violations have derivative-process feasibility certificates, because a latent response path imposes cone restrictions on (p',F'_{Y,D|Z}) beyond pointwise envelope bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Toru Kitagawa
anchor_paper.claim: Instrument validity restrictions including monotonicity have testable implications in heterogeneous treatment-effect models with discrete instruments.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked because continuous derivative processes add function-space cone restrictions absent from finite inequalities.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Kitagawa2015 because continuous instruments can be discretized and tested pairwise.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: pairwise discretization does not solve the common response-path compatibility or derivative-cone necessity/sufficiency problem.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2021 has established that partial monotonicity can restore positive-weight IV interpretation with multiple instruments. Our seed conjectures that scalar continuous-IV envelope monotonicity has a different positive-weight object, because the negative derivative density is calibrated along one margin rather than removed by instrument-by-instrument sign restrictions.

anchor_paper.author: Magne Mogstad, Alexander Torgovitsky, and Christopher R. Walters
anchor_paper.claim: Standard monotonicity can fail with multiple instruments, motivating partial-monotonicity conditions for positive-weight IV interpretation.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment-process/monotonicity variation pattern, not blocked because the scalar derivative envelope produces a signed sharp set, not a multiple-IV positive-weight restatement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2021 because partial monotonicity already handles monotonicity failures.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their setting is multiple instruments and positive weights, not scalar continuous-IV derivative-envelope sharp bounds.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DeChaisemartin2017 has established that LATE-like effects without full monotonicity require extra structure tying defiers to compliers. Our seed conjectures that a bounded continuous-IV defier density gives sharp intervals without tying their outcomes, because the envelope controls mass while leaving treatment-effect heterogeneity adversarial.

anchor_paper.author: Clément de Chaisemartin
anchor_paper.claim: Some LATE-like effects can be recovered without full monotonicity only under additional structure tying defiers to compliers.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens/additional-structure pattern, not blocked because it replaces point-identifying structure with a sharp adversarial interval and a calibrated density envelope.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DeChaisemartin2017 because he already studies LATE without monotonicity.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: that paper recovers effects under extra defier/complier structure; it does not characterize sharp bounded-density continuous-IV intervals with arbitrary defier outcomes.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Kitagawa2015 would imply that monotonicity failures can be detected by finite conditional-distribution inequalities in discrete IV models. Our seed conjectures that continuous-IV envelope refutability is governed by derivative cone inequalities, because infinitesimal response signs must integrate into a feasible bounded-variation binary path.

anchor_paper.author: Toru Kitagawa
anchor_paper.claim: Instrument validity restrictions including monotonicity have testable implications in heterogeneous treatment-effect models with discrete instruments.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends discrete to continuous pattern, not blocked because the extension introduces a new derivative-cone/refutability object and global BV compatibility.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Kitagawa2015 by taking a fine partition of the continuous instrument.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: a fine partition gives necessary finite checks but not a closed continuous cone or dual certificate for the envelope model.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HeckmanVytlacil2005 has established pointwise LIV/MTE identification under monotone threshold crossing. Our seed conjectures that the bounded-violation sharp set converges to the LIV formula in Hausdorff distance as b -> 0 only under first-stage strength, because weak p' regions can amplify vanishing negative response mass.

anchor_paper.author: James J. Heckman and Edward Vytlacil
anchor_paper.claim: MTE unifies standard treatment-effect parameters and uses the local instrumental-variable derivative as the observed-data counterpart under the latent-index model.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/phase-transition pattern, not blocked because it proposes a sharp continuity modulus and weak-first-stage failure boundary, not a routine limiting statement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from HeckmanVytlacil2005 because setting violations to zero recovers LIV.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: exact zero recovery is routine, but Hausdorff continuity and weak-first-stage modulus for positive b_n are not in the LIV theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1 asserted strict pairwise-versus-global tightening only as a J=2 witness problem. Our seed conjectures that the continuum analogue creates a mesh-refinement strictness theorem, because adjacent continuous-IV envelopes cannot be optimized independently when one response path must serve all margins.

anchor_paper.author: ThmSmith prior proposal pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1
anchor_paper.claim: Strict tightening is asserted only as an existence phenomenon in the J=2 witness class; reviewer requested a rational witness template.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: T=finite-to-continuum pattern, not blocked because the strictness is a path-compatibility phase phenomenon, not a routine generalization.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the ordinal proposal because continuous instruments are limits of ordinal grids.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the prior proposal left the finite witness open and did not prove mesh-refinement or continuous-envelope sharpness.

proposed_from.literature_map:
ImbensAngrist1994 (Econometrica) is the foundational LATE point-identification result and makes monotonicity the key causal-content restriction.
Vytlacil2002 (Econometrica) equates LATE monotonicity with a latent-index threshold-crossing model.
HeckmanVytlacil2005 (Econometrica) supplies the continuous-IV MTE/LIV derivative formula that the zero-violation regime should recover.
FlorensHeckmanMeghirVytlacil2008 (Econometrica) is an adjacent continuous-treatment/control-function anchor.
Klein2010 (Journal of Econometrics) directly studies IV without monotonicity and frames monotonicity departures as bias/contamination.
ShaikhVytlacil2011 (Econometrica) and MachadoShaikhVytlacil2019 (Journal of Econometrics) are partial-ID IV anchors for triangular/binary-treatment settings and sign information.
DeChaisemartin2017 (Quantitative Economics) shows LATE-like interpretation without full monotonicity requires additional defier/complier structure.
MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018 (Econometrica) formulates MTE policy-effect partial identification through optimization over restrictions.
Kitagawa2015 (Econometrica) gives testable implications of IV validity/monotonicity in discrete heterogeneous-effect models.
MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2021 (AER) and MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024 (Journal of Econometrics) develop partial monotonicity and multiple-IV MTE policy evaluation, but not scalar continuous-IV derivative-envelope bounds.
Mountjoy2024 (Journal of Econometrics) gives a recent vector-monotonicity relaxation for multiple instruments.
JiangSunHu2024PartialMonotonicityTest (Economics Letters) extends testing to partial monotonicity.
Goff2022 studies monotonicity-violation sensitivity for policy-relevant parameters but not a sharp continuous-IV derivative-envelope identified set.
